title: Reverse-engineering corpus manifest
description: Provenance + license ledger for every source the reverse-engineering program studies. Lives at _corpus/manifest.json (gitignored). distill.py enforces the per-record public_reuse_policy before emitting any public artifact.

record_id.description: Stable slug, e.g. 'f1000_review_2024_dta_calibration'. Used by distill.py to authorize reuse.

doi.description: DOI if the source has one; null for DOI-less reports.

source_type.description: oa_article = open-access paper; open_review = published peer-review report.

license.description: SPDX-style id or short name, e.g. 'CC-BY-4.0', 'CC-BY-NC-ND-4.0', 'CC0-1.0', or 'unknown'.

license_url.description: Link to the license text or the page asserting the license. Use '' only when license is 'unknown'.

retrieved_at.description: YYYY-MM-DD the source was fetched into _corpus/.

verbatim_allowed.description: Whether any verbatim quotation may appear in a committed artifact. Default false. Set true only for confirmed CC-BY / CC0 sources, and even then prefer synthesis.

public_reuse_policy.description: synthetic_only = only freshly-authored, non-derivative output may be committed; paraphrase_ok = close paraphrase permitted (requires a permissive license); cc_by_attribution = CC-BY/CC0 content may be reused with attribution.

attribution.description: Required citation line when public_reuse_policy is cc_by_attribution. Optional otherwise.

notes.description: Free-text: why this license was assigned, what was learned, caveats.

linked_artifacts.description: Data/code availability artifacts linked from the article (supplementary files, GitHub/GitLab repos, Zenodo/OSF/Figshare deposits, HuggingFace model/dataset cards). Each carries its OWN license, which is verified independently of the article — a code repo's software license (MIT/Apache/GPL/none) and a Zenodo deposit's per-deposit license differ from the article's. distill.py validates each like a record and authorizes its reuse separately (RECORD_ID#INDEX).

artifact_type.description: supplementary = supplementary files (usually inherit the article license — verify); code_repo = source-code repository (its own software license); dataset = data deposit (Zenodo/OSF/Figshare, per-deposit license); model_card / preprint / protocol / other.

doi.description: DOI of the artifact if it has one (e.g., a Zenodo deposit DOI); null otherwise.

license.description: The artifact's OWN license, verified independently. SPDX-style id or short name (e.g., 'MIT', 'Apache-2.0', 'GPL-3.0', 'CC0-1.0', 'CC-BY-4.0', or 'unknown').

license_url.description: Link to the artifact's license (e.g., the repo LICENSE file or the Zenodo deposit page). Use '' only when license is 'unknown'.

verbatim_allowed.description: Whether verbatim reuse of this artifact (e.g., copying a code snippet or a figure) may appear in a committed artifact. Default false.

public_reuse_policy.description: Same semantics as the record's policy, applied to this artifact. Default synthetic_only. A non-default policy requires a verified permissive license on the artifact (a permissive software license — MIT/Apache/BSD/CC0 — for a code_repo; CC-BY/CC0 for data/figures).

attribution.description: Required citation/attribution line when public_reuse_policy is cc_by_attribution. Optional otherwise.

notes.description: Free-text: what the artifact added (e.g., the released training code revealed the real threshold / leakage; the supplementary held the calibration plot), and license caveats.
